reasoning:
Mosaic recently released Q3 2025 earnings which triggered a significant short-term stock slide (down 13% over an 8-day losing streak through Nov 6) due to a revenue miss and guidance that 2025 free cash flows would be "very close to zero." This establishes strong negative momentum and bearish post-earnings announcement drift (PEAD). 

For the 1-week horizon (Nov 16), the short-term negative trend and analyst downgrades skew the probability negatively to 0.44. 
For the 1-month horizon (Dec 9), this downward bias is somewhat balanced by standard mean-reversion, but the company also historically goes ex-dividend in early December (predictably dropping the stock price by its roughly $0.22 dividend). Thus, the probability increases toward the base rate but remains below it at 0.47.
By the 3-month horizon (Feb 7), the initial post-earnings momentum largely decays and broad equity risk premium (positive drift) takes over, returning the probability very close to 0.50 (estimated at 0.49), strictly satisfying the required increase in uncertainty for distant forecast horizons.
